headline: Where did the tradition of Visita Iglesia come from? | ABS-CBN News
text:
Catholics light candles during their Visita Iglesia at the Antipolo Cathedral on Maundy Thursday, March 29, 2018. Filipinos take a break from work to commemorate the life, death, and resurrection of Jesus through various acts or resting and taking a vacation. Mark Demayo, ABS-CBN News/File
MANILA -- One of the most famous Lenten traditions of Filipinos is the pilgrimage of "Visita Iglesia," where Catholics visit seven churches to pray.
This is commonly done during Maundy Thursdays, so Catholics can see the Altar of Repose of each church, but over the years, more Filipinos have also done it during the days leading to the Holy Week.
But where did this tradition come from?
According to church historian Aaron Veloso, the tradition dates back to the 1500's, starting with Saint Philip Neri.
"Nung isang Jubilee Year sa Rome nung 1500s, merong dineklara ang Papa na pitong basilica na nirerecommend niya sa pilgrims to gain plenary indulgence," Veloso said.
"In 1633, naisip ni Saint Philip Neri na baka yung pilgrims, magandang puntahan ang seven churches na yon," he added.
(During one Jubilee year in Rome in the 1500s, the Pope declared 7 basilica he recommended to pilgrims to gain plenary indulgence. In 1633, St. Philip Neri thought that the pilgrims may go to those 7 Churches.) 
READ: Pilgrimage sites in Rizal Province to visit this Holy Week
The seven major churches include Saint Peter's Basilica, Saint Paul's Basilica, Saint Mary Major, Saint John Lateran, Saint Sebastian, Saint Lawrence, and the Basilica of the Holy Cross.
Catholics would travel far to these churches for indulgences, or the cleansing from temporal punishment.
During the Spanish colonial rule, this tradition arrived in the Philippines starting in Intramuros, which coincidentally had seven churches, including the now Manila Cathedral and San Agustin Church.
"The concept of going to pilgrimage is rampant, may mga indulgences na binibigay ang simbahan para sa mga nagpa-participate in pious traditions like that," Veloso said.
(There are indulgences that the Church does for people who participate in pious traditions like that.) 
As modern transportation bloomed, the tradition spread to other parts of the country as it became easier to travel to various churches.
During the 70s, the tradition grew, and Filipinos visited 14 churches that signify the 14 Stations of the Cross.
In Manila, common churches visited during the Visita Iglesia season include the Quiapo Church, San Sebastian Church, and the Santo Domingo Church.
Churches will be usually packed with pilgrims during the Holy Week, with throngs of devotees earnestly praying for their intentions.
For Veloso, Visita Iglesia has a deeper meaning for Filipinos.
"We are not foreign to the concept of suffering. We find the passion and death of Christ as something very similar to our lives as Filipinos," he said.
LOOK: Pilgrims visit Regina RICA as Holy Week nears
SOLEMN JOURNEY
For devout Catholic Francis Santos, Visita Iglesia has become an annual tradition, and the Lenten season will not be complete without this pilgrimage.
"Simula bata pa ako, kinamulatan ko na mag Visita Iglesia, simula pa noong panahon ng Lolo at Lola ko," Santos said.
(Ever since I was a kid, I have been doing the "Visita Iglesia," ever since the time of my grandparents.) 
They have traveled to various provinces like Bulacan, Bataan, and Rizal to keep this tradition alive, seeing churches built from different eras but mostly with colorful histories.
"Every year, iba-ibang probinsya ang pinupuntahan, so for this year, Nueva Ecija naman yung napili naming puntahan," he added.
(We go to different provinces every year. This year, we decided to go to Nueva Ecija.) 
He said he finds it very fascinating to see many Filipinos participate in the tradition of sacrifice.
"For me, parang nakikiisa ka sa buhay, kamatayan, pagpapakasakit at muling pagkabuhay ng Panginoon," he said.
"Isa siyang banal na gawain, isa siyang banal na paglalakbay," he added.
(For me, you are commemorating the life, death, the suffering and the resurrection of the Lord.)
READ: Cybercrime experts warn of 14 travel scams ahead of Holy Week, summer break
FROM THE ARCHIVES:

headline: Turkey, Nutella maker Ferrero resolve hazelnut price complaints | ABS-CBN News
text:
Nutella peanut butter jars are pictured on a shelf at a supermarket in Hamburg, Germany, 17 November 2011.  EPA/MARCUS BRANDT


ISTANBUL - Turkey's competition watchdog has closed an investigation into Italian company Ferrero after the Nutella maker made commitments to resolve complaints from local hazelnut farmers that it was abusing dominant position to underpay them.
The Turkish Competition Authority had opened the probe against the company in 2022 after farmers said Ferrero was paying meagre sums for their hazelnuts and then selling its products at significantly higher prices.
"The investigation against (Ferrero's Turkish subsidiary) has been concluded with commitments that have resolved the competition problems that were raised," the competition authority said in a statement on Thursday.
Ferrero said Friday it "welcomes the closure of the investigation and confirms its commitment to operate in Turkey by buying in-shell hazelnuts not below the intervention reference price."
The Turkish Competition Authority had opened the investigation into the Italian giant, which also makes Ferrero Rocher chocolates, in 2022.
Ferrero in 2022 employed more than 1,000 people in Turkey, the world's largest hazelnut producer, where the company has been sourcing hazelnuts for more than three decades.
In 2023, Turkey exported more than 280,000 tonnes of hazelnuts, worth $1.86 billion, according to the trade association.
rba/ach/nmc-lth/gv
© Agence France-Presse

headline: PH sets world record on biggest human lung formation | ABS-CBN News
text:
The country is poised to break the Guinness World Record for the largest human lung formation in the Quirino Grandstand. Courtesy: Department of Health
MANILA (UPDATED) – The Philippines on Saturday set the Guinness World Record for the biggest human lung formation, beating the previous mark set by India.
For the lung formation earlier today, the Philippines had 5,596 participants, beating India's 5,007. India's record was in 2017. 
The feat came on the commemoration of World Tuberculosis (TB) Day this year at the Quirino Grandstand.
Chong Wai Yip, an adjudicator at the Guinness World Records, said in order to set a new record, they considered the number of participants, the form of the human lung, and there should be no gap during the official recording.
Guinness gave three attempts. The Philippines was successful in setting the new record after its second attempt.

One of the participants, Oswald Macapagal, said he was excited to join the event. He was at the Quirino Grandstand before 5 a.m. to participate in the activity. 
READ: DOH reports increase in TB cases in 2023
"Gumising kami ng umaga kasi excited kami pumunta… dahil malaki ang maibabahagi namin especially hindi lang sa Pilipinas kundi sa whole world. Ang TB ay kailangan natin labanan para na rin sa health natin," said Macapagal. 
Health Secretary Ted Herbosa said he was happy his agency and the Philippines made it. He also reminded Filipinos the importance of getting checked for tuberculosis, as this was the advocacy of their event. 
The Philippines has set a new Guinness World Record on the biggest human lung formation, March 16, 2024. Job Manahan, ABS-CBN News
"Pinakita ng mga kababayan nating Pilipino na kapag tayo ay may gustuhin – mag-break ng Guinness World Record… the idea is World TB Day, kailangan malaman ng tao na  mayroon pa ring sakit na TB. Walang stigma diyan. Nagagamot ang TB," said the health chief. 
"Ang ginagawa natin ay hindi  lang sa Pilipinas, para sa buong mundo. Ang TB ay hindi lang problema sa Pilipinas, problema ito sa buong mundo," he added. 
TB cases in the Philippines surged in 2023, Herbosa earlier said. 
A total of 612,000 TB cases were reported in 2023. This is equivalent to approximately 549 cases per 100,000 population, marking an increase from 439 cases per 100,000 in 2022.
He stressed the importance of seeking prompt X-ray and molecular confirmation for individuals with symptoms, emphasizing the critical need for timely medication.
READ: Primary health goals of PH 'achievable' - USAID official

RELATED VIDEOS:

headline: 5 million at risk of starvation in Sudan: UN | ABS-CBN News
text:
A young boy showing signs of malnutrition wanders amidst ruins in Rumbek's central market, 21 January 2004. EPA/EDWARD PARSONS
UNITED NATIONS, United States - The United Nations appealed Friday for Sudan's battling factions to allow delivery of humanitarian relief to fend off looming "catastrophic" hunger.
Some five million Sudanese could face calamitous food insecurity in coming months as a nearly yearlong war between rival generals continues to tear the country apart, according to a UN document seen Friday by AFP.



The war between army chief Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and his former deputy, Mohamed Hamdan Daglo, has since April last year killed tens of thousands, destroyed infrastructure and crippled the economy.
It has also triggered a dire humanitarian crisis and acute food shortages, with the country teetering on the brink of famine.
Noting that some 18 million Sudanese are already facing acute food insecurity -- a record during harvest season -- UN humanitarian chief Martin Griffiths warned in a letter to the Security Council that "almost 5 million people could slip into catastrophic food insecurity in some parts of the country in the coming months."
He noted that nearly 730,000 Sudanese children -- including more than 240,000 in Darfur -- are thought to suffer from "severe" malnutrition.
"Aid organizations require safe, rapid, sustained and unimpeded access -- including across conflict lines within Sudan," said UN Secretary General Antonio Guterres's spokesman Stephane Dujarric.
"A massive mobilization of resources from the international community is also critical," he added.

The UN's World Food Programme has warned that the war risks "triggering the world's largest hunger crisis."
Jill Lawler, the emergency chief in Sudan for the UN children's agency UNICEF, said there were enough aid stocks in Port Sudan, but the problem was getting the aid from there to the people in need.
Lawler said she last week had led the first UN mission to reach Khartoum state since war erupted 11 months ago.
They had seen first-hand that "the scale and magnitude of needs for children across the country are simply staggering," she told reporters in Geneva via video link from New York.
The war "is pushing the country towards a famine" with hunger "the number one concern people expressed."
MOMENT OF TRUTH
Mandeep O'Brien, UNICEF representative in Sudan, said 14 million children needed humanitarian aid and four million were displaced.
There was only a "small window left to prevent mass loss of children's lives and future," she warned on X, formerly Twitter.
World Health Organization regional director Hanan Balkhy, who recently returned from a trip to Sudan, underlined the acute needs in Darfur, saying most health facilities were looted, damaged or destroyed.
Griffiths, the UN aid chief, lamented that fighting continued to rage during the Muslim holy month of Ramadan despite a Security Council resolution calling for a cessation of hostilities.
"This is a moment of truth," he wrote on X, formerly Twitter. "The parties must silence the guns, protect civilians and ensure humanitarian access."
The UN on Friday called for more financial support for aid operations in Sudan.
UN spokeswoman Alessandra Vellucci told reporters in Geneva that the world body had appealed for $2.7 billion to provide aid this year, but had received just five percent of that amount so far.
apo-abd/imm/jj/tjj/acb
© Agence France-Presse

headline: Remains of teen Fil-Am found dead in New Jersey to be brought back to PH | ABS-CBN News
text:
The remains of the missing Filipino-American teen found dead in Newark Bay, New Jersey will be brought back home to the Philippines, her family said.
The body of 18-year-old Hayley Erin “Lee” Avestruz Alvarado was pulled out of Newark Bay in Bayonne, New Jersey on March 5.  
According to her father Stephen, they will hold a wake for Hayley on March 18, while funeral services and cremation will be held on March 19 in Jersey City.
The Alvarado family will then bring Hayley's remains back home to their loved ones in the Philippines.
In an earlier interview with ABS-CBN News, Stephen said on December 17, Hayley was seen in their security camera recording leaving their home past midnight, wearing her back pack. That was the last time they saw her alive.  
Hayley was a freshman at Montclair University studying language engineering, majoring in linguistics. She graduated summa cum laude in high school.  
“Si Haley, talagang matalino siya sa school, focused talaga siya sa pag-aaral, nakikita ko sa kanya yung mga achievements niya gusto niya talaga palagi number one siya sa klase,” Stephen said.
He also admitted he was so busy at work that he wished he had spent more time with his daughter.  
“Ang nangyayayri sa amin dito, Trabaho, trabaho, trabaho -- I can say na may pagkukulang ako na, hindi ko sila mabigyan ng oras,” he said, "Kung ibabalik ulit, gusto kong yakapin siya araw araw,” Stephen said.
He also told ABS-CBN News that based on Hayley’s search history on her computer about bus routes, she may have taken the bus after leaving their home on December 17.  
Stephen said that according to investigators, Hayley was last seen in security cameras in South Kearny, near the Hackensack River -- just 17 minutes away from their Jersey City home, if she took the NJ Transit bus number 1.    
The part of Hackensack River where she was last seen is about 4.6 miles away upstream from Newark Bay -- where her body was recently found.  
Authorities said there were no visible signs of trauma on her body.  
The cause of death is still pending, as the family is still waiting for the autopsy report from the medical examiner.
Missing 18-year-old FilAm found dead in NJ bay
RELATED VIDEO

headline: Fil-Am legal expert discusses Georgia election case dev't vs. Trump | ABS-CBN News
text:
A major defeat for former U.S. president Donald Trump was handed down on Friday by a Fulton County judge in Georgia. 
Judge Scott McAfee ruled that District Attorney Fani Willis can continue with prosecuting Trump and his allies over their alleged interference with the 2020 election results in the state, if Willis removes Special Prosecutor Nathan Wade. 
Trump’s lawyers wanted Willis disqualified from the criminal case or have the charges thrown out after the discovery of a romantic relationship between Willis and Wade, which they said was a conflict of interest.  
They also argued that Willis financially benefited from hiring Wade. Wade quit as special prosecutor shortly after the said ruling. 
“If she (Willis) were to have been removed from the case, that would disqualify her entire office,” said Carl Cansino, a FilAm former assistant district attorney in Georgia, “because her office is considered like a law firm. And so, [if] one lawyer is disqualified from the case, the entire office is disqualified.”
In his ruling, McAfee said Willis simply made bad choices. Cansino, who is now a criminal defense lawyer, said that had Willis been disqualified, the criminal case against Trump and his co-defendants would be assigned to another office.  
“The prosecuting attorneys council will appoint another prosecutor from another judicial circuit to take the place of that disqualified prosecutor,” he said. “And then, the new prosecutor, whoever is assigned, has ultimate authority to do whatever he or she desires with the case.”
Trump’s attorneys have said they will look at legal options to continue to fight the case including a possible appeal. 
“But in this particular case, I don't think that there's any real novel issue to be heard by the Court of Appeals,” Cansino added.
If there’s no appeal, and despite Wade’s removal, Cansino said that more than likely, the criminal case against Trump and his co-defendants can play out this summer.  
Trump and 18 of his allies were indicted last year on state charges from their alleged efforts to overturn Georgia's 2020 election results.
Four of the 18 have already accepted plea deals in the case in exchange for their testimony.

headline: Fil-Canadian BC lawmaker denies antisemitism claims | ABS-CBN News
text:
Filipino-Canadian lawmaker Mable Elmore has defended herself from antisemitism claims alleged by her fellow lawmaker after she called for a ceasefire to the ongoing war between Israel and Hamas.
Elmore’s speech at the BC legislature in November reportedly alarmed the Jewish community in Vancouver, so much so that they asked for her resignation as parliamentary secretary for anti-racism initiatives.
Jewish lawmaker Selina Robinson said it was the last straw for their community. She objected to Elmore’s remarks which supposedly faulted only Israel for the infighting in Gaza. 
“What the Jewish community took offense to was that she didn't qualify,” said Robinson. “It didn't mention that Hamas had attacked Israel, and slaughtered 1200 people, children, raped women mutilated them, and that this was a response to that.”
She added: “So, her (Elmore) statements suggested that Israel was the sole aggressor in this conflict and that was very upsetting to the Jewish community because it wasn't a full statement that explained the whole story.”
But Elmore maintained that her comments were balanced, and reaffirmed her position for a two-state solution to the conflict. 
“Calling for peace in the region on all sides, I think it's the way to move forward,” she said. 
Robinson traced the Jewish leaders’ mistrust of Elmore from what they perceive as antisemitic comments that Elmore made in an interview back in 2004.
Elmore said she understands that as an elected official, past comments she made will always be dug up. But she added that she has continued to reach out to the Jewish community in Vancouver.
“I had a very good meeting with them last week on a number of services, the Jewish community, are very active in the community,” Elmore said.” They do a lot, not just for the Jewish community, but for all you know, British Columbians.”
Robinson, however, said she is not aware of any recent meeting between Elmore and the Jewish community nor has Elmore reached out to her.
The Coquitlam-Maillardville lawmaker recently quit the ruling BC NDP over what she said were instances of antisemitism within the party.
She also accused her former party of double standards in dealing with her and their other members holding government posts.
While was forced to resign as a cabinet minister for her earlier comments that angered the Muslim community, Elmore still holds the position of parliamentary secretary.
“If someone says something that hurts the community and you want to remove them, then you do that for everybody,” said Robinson. “If Mabel wasn't removed, then why was I removed?”
Responding, Elmore said: “That's her perspective. I don't agree with that. I don't see that; I haven't experienced that but certainly that's her perspective. Our whole caucus is really committed to human rights and standing against hate.”
Following Robinson’s allegations against the NDP, BC Premier David Eby met with Jewish leaders last week.
In a statement, the Jewish Federation of Greater Vancouver demanded that the premier act now on the alarming increase of antisemitic incidents in B.C.
Robinson further alleged that even in schools, young Jewish students are being singled out by teachers to explain to the class what’s happening in Gaza.
“They're being held to account for the action of the Israeli government,” she said. “Can you imagine asking Chinese students or Filipino students to be accountable for the actions of the Filipino government? We would never do that.”
The Vancouver police reported that since the Hamas attack last Oct. 7, there has been a 62-percent increase in incidents of antisemitism in the city.

headline: Magnitude 4.4 quake strikes off Surigao del Sur | ABS-CBN News
text:
A magnitude 4.4 earthquake struck off Surigao del Sur on Saturday night.
The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said the tremor occurred 18 kilometers northeast of Cortes, Surigao del Sur.
It took place at 8:28 p.m. at a depth of 31 kilometers.
Intensity I was felt in the following areas:

The quake hit the province two months after a magnitude 5.7 earthquake struck the same province.

headline: Marcos Jr. back in Manila after Central Europe visit | ABS-CBN News
text:
President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. returned to the Philippines on Saturday after a nearly week-long visit to Central Europe.
 During his arrival speech at the Villamor Airbase in Pasay City, Marcos described both his working visit to Germany and state visit to the Czech Republic as a success.
“I’m confident that these visits to Germany and the Czech Republic has strengthened our bonds to these two countries in Central Europe and created opportunities for cooperation and prosperity,” he said.
Marcos touted the $4 billion or P220 billion worth of investment pledges he brought home from Berlin. He said once these pledges are realized, they will benefit the sectors of renewable energy, manufacturing, healthcare, innovation and startups, IT-BPM, minerals processing, agriculture, and aerospace.
Meanwhile, Marcos said the joint communique signed between the Department of Migrant Workers and the Czech labor ministry in Prague will help ensure that the rights of overseas Filipino workers in the country are protected.
He said the work continues for a Philippines-European Union (EU) Free Trade Agreement.
“Germany and the Czech Republic have also expressed their valuable support for the resumption of the Philippines-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations. In this regard, I have authorized DTI Secretary Alfredo Pascual to make the necessary follow up on the progress of all FTA discussions at the EU headquarters in Brussels,” said Marcos.
Marcos ended both his trips to Germany and the Czech Republic by meeting with the Filipino community in each country.
He said they are among the main reasons why his administration is determined to score more foreign investments.
“I assured our countrymen of this government’s unwavering commitment to creating more job opportunities and improving our economy, ensuring that they can return home to the Philippines and earn a livelihood without having to sacrifice their time with their loved ones,” he said.
Marcos’ visit to Central Europe is his fifth international trip this 2024.
BEARING FRUIT  
Amid criticism of the President’s jet-setting ways, Philippine Economic Zone Authority (PEZA) Director General Tereso Panga said Marcos’ approach has been “very effective” in bringing in investors.
In a separate news forum on Saturday, Panga said out of the P175 billion in foreign investments that PEZA approved in 2023, 43 percent or P75 billion can be attributed to Marcos’ travels.
“I would say that it's because of the President reaching out to different economies and taking advantage of our FTAs, free trade agreements, that we as a nation are able to attract also investments,” said Panga.

headline: Iba pang establisimyento sa Chocolate Hills, tiwalang 'di maipapasara | ABS-CBN News

headline: Suspek sa pagpatay sa mag-inang balikbayan, sumuko | ABS-CBN News
text:
Nitong Huwebes ng hapon, nadiskubre ang mga katawan ng mag-ina na inilibing sa bakanteng lote hindi kalayuan sa bahay ng kapatid. Quezon PNP handout 
Hawak ng Tayabas City police ang kapatid na babaeng suspek sa pagpatay at paglilibing sa mag-inang balikbayan sa Tayabas City.
Kusang loob na sumuko ang kapatid na babae ng nanay na  biktima sa bayan ng Pio Duran sa Albay nitong tanghali ng Biyernes.
Sa panayam kay Police Maj. Darwin Sevilla, hepe ng Pio Duran, sinabi nito na humingi sa kanila ng tulong ang anak na lalaki ng suspek upang maproseso ang pagsuko ng kanyang nanay, na  kapatid ng biktimang si Lorry Litada.
Kaagad silang nakipag-ugnayan sa Tayabas City police at itinurn-over ang ginang.
Nitong gabi ng Biyernes ay dinala na rin sa Tayabas City ang suspek para sa proper na imbestigasyon.
Ayon sa  naunang report ng Tayabas Police, nawala ang mag-asawang may-ari ng isang bahay sa Bella Vita Subdivision, Brgy. Isabang, Tayabas City noong unang linggo nitong Marso, matapos na maiulat na nawawala ang mga biktimang mag-inang OFW na sina Lorry Litada at ang Japanese citizen na anak nito na si Mai Motegi, 26-anyos, noong February 21.
Nagbalikbayan ang mag-inang biktima mula sa Japan noong February 20 at nakituloy sa bahay ng mga suspek.
Nitong Huwebes ng hapon, nadiskubre ang mga katawan ng mag-ina na inilibing sa bakanteng lote hindi kalayuan sa bahay ng kapatid.
Bangkay ng mag-inang balikbayan, natagpuan sa bakanteng lote sa Tayabas
Natuklasan din na nawawala ang 5 milyong pisong pera ng mag-ina na ipambabayad sa kanilang nabiling property sa isang bayan sa Quezon.
Itinuring ng Tayabas PNP na "person of interest" ang kapatid nito at ang mister at ang pera ang motibo sa krimen.
Ayon pa kay Sevilla, tubong Jovillar, Albay  ang babaeng suspek at nakapag-asawa naman ang anak nito  ng taga Pio Duran kaya doon ang mga ito pansamantalang tumuloy nang umalis ng Tayabas City.
Nasa kustodiya na ng Tayabas police ang suspek habang patuloy ang isinasagawang imbestigasyon.

headline: Bullet-riddled bodies of 3 men found in Bulacan | ABS-CBN News
text:
The bodies of 3 men with bullet wounds were found on a tricycle in Purok 7, Barangay Basuit, San Ildefonso, Bulacan on Saturday morning, local authorities said.
According to barangay officials, they received a report from a concerned resident who heard gunshots.
Gilbert Cruz, a barangay councilor, recalled, "Nung dumating po kami sa area nakita po namin 'yung tricycle na nakabaliktad...Nung dumating po kami is 'yung isa humihinga pa, sumisigaw pa ng tulong.. Di po namin kilala ang mga tao dahil hindi po namin sila namumukaan." (When we arrived at the area, we saw the tricycle overturned. When we arrived, one of them was still breathing, still shouting for help... We don't know the people because we don't recognize their faces.)
The San Ildefonso Municipal Police revealed that a vehicle was seen chasing the tricycle.
However, police opted not to release the CCTV footage to prevent compromising the ongoing investigation.
"Tatlo sila sa tricycle na nakasakay at hinahabol ng isang SUV or pickup. Nung inabot na sa purok siyete ng brgy ay tumaob yung sasakyan nila yung tricycle and then yung humabol na vehicle is bumaba sa sasakyan pinutukan yung tatlong biktima," said Police Lt. Col. Russell Dennis Reburiano, chief of police of the San Ildefonso Municipal Police Station.
Theft of electricity cables is being considered as a potential motive for the shooting, police said.
"Last week merong mga nanakawan ng kable ng kuryente dun sa may mga farm tska dun sa mga residente. Ngayon itong tricycle na to na nahagip sa CCTV na parang 'yun yung dumaan sa kanilang barangay na gumagala. Sa loob ng tricycle nakakita kami ng rolyo ng mga pinutol na kable ng kuryente," Reburiano said. (Last week, there were reports of theft of electricity cables in the farms and among residents. Now, this tricycle captured on CCTV appears to have passed through their barangay, carrying a roll of cut electricity cables)
The aunt of one of the victims expressed disbelief over the fate of her 21-year-old nephew.
"Hindi po namin kasi namin kilala yung mga kasama namin na yan basta ang alam po namin nagtatrabaho siya. Sinisigurado po namin sa inyo na talagang nandun siya sa bahay namin wala siyang ginagawang hindi maganda lalo na pagnanakaw ng kable na yan," she said. (We don't know his companions, but what we know is he was working. We assure you he was at home, not doing anything wrong, especially stealing cables.)
She appealed for justice for her relative. "Sana nga po kung may maling nagawa, sana po di nila ginanyan para pong hayop lang na pinatay. Kung may pagkakamali pwde namang pagsisihan pwde namang ikulong napakabata pa jusko. Paano kung nadamay lang po yung pamangkin namin? Sana naawa man lang sila nagkaroon man lang ng konting habag." (I hope that if they did something wrong, I hope they didn't do it like they were just killing an animal. If there was a mistake, they could have regretted it, they could have been imprisoned, they're still young, my goodness. What if our nephew was just caught up in it? I hope they at least had some pity, at least showed a little mercy.)
Police are still investigating the case to identify the assailants.

headline: South Korean senior doctors to resign in support of junior medics' walkout | ABS-CBN News
text:
South Korean Medical professors hold up signs protesting the government's plan to augment medical student admissions at Jeonbuk National University in Jeonju, on March 13, 2024. EPA-EFE/YONHAP 
A group of South Korean senior doctors said Saturday that they would resign starting March 25 in support of junior medics in a nearly month-long strike over government training reforms that has plunged hospitals into chaos.
Thousands of trainee doctors stopped working on February 20 to protest government reforms aimed at easing doctor shortages by increasing the number of medical students -- which the medics claim is the final straw for overworked and underpaid early career professionals.
Crucial surgeries and treatments have been cancelled, but the government says the country has so far avoided a full-blown crisis thanks in part to nurses and senior doctors stepping up, as well as military medics who have been sent in to help.
Representatives of medical professors at 20 universities -- who are also senior doctors at general hospitals -- held a meeting late Friday, with those at 16 institutions "overwhelmingly in favour" of supporting their junior colleagues, said Bang Jae-seung, the head of the group.
Professors at "each university have decided to voluntarily submit resignation letters starting from March 25th", Bang told reporters Saturday.
But "we have reached a consensus that until the resignation is finalised, each individual should do their best in the treatment of patients in their respective positions, just as they have done so far", he added.
Bang did not disclose the exact number of professors expected to walk off the job on March 25th.
The health ministry this week reiterated that negotiations over the reform plans were out of the question, saying it would implement the healthcare reforms "without wavering".
The government has ordered junior medics to return or face legal action, and has moved to suspend the medical licences of those who refuse to comply, while offering incentives and setting up a hotline to support any who defy the walkout.
Seoul is pushing to admit 2,000 more students to medical schools annually from next year to address what it says is one of the lowest doctor-to-population ratios among developed nations.
Bang said Saturday that doctors cannot agree to an increase of 2,000 more students under any circumstances.
"We request the government once again. Please reconsider the figure of 2,000. Without this, initiating any negotiation will be impossible," he said.
"If the current situation continues, it won't be long before university hospitals collapse, dealing a significant and long-lasting blow to our country's medical system," he added.
Doctors say they fear the reform will erode the quality of service and medical education, but proponents of the plan accuse them of trying to safeguard their salaries and social status.
This week, the junior doctors said they had submitted a "letter requesting emergency intervention" from the International Labour Organization (ILO), claiming they were being "forced" by the government into unwanted labour. The government has dismissed the claim.
The reform plan enjoys broad public support, but a new poll by local media found 34 percent of people wanted the government to negotiate to end the standoff.

headline: 2 suspects arrested in killing of broadcaster Jumalon | ABS-CBN News
text:
Two suspects in the killing of Misamis Occidental broadcaster Juan “DJ Johnny Walker” Jumalon have been arrested, authorities said Saturday.
The Presidential Task Force on Media Security (PTFoMS) announced the arrest of cousins Boboy Sagaray Bongcawel and Renante "Inteng" Saja Bongcawel.

The suspects, implicated in the November 5, 2023 killing, are residents of Barangay Manla.

The suspects were arrested on Friday in Barangay Poblacion by joint operatives of the Calamba and Sapang Dalaga Municipal Police Stations by virtue of an arrest warrant for murder and theft issued by Calamba Executive Judge Michael Lotao Ajoc of Regional Trial Court Branch 36, 10th Judicial Region.  
Boboy was the person identified poking a gun at Jumalon's radio station helper while Inteng was identified as the driver of their getaway motorcycle.
PTFoMS executive director Usec Paul Gutierrez expressed confidence that police will also be able to arrest the remaining suspect Julito "Ricky" Mangumpit.
Mangumpit was tagged as the gunman who shot Jumalon.
"Already, the Misamis Occidental PPO is following up on several leads on Mangumpit's whereabouts," he said.
A reward of P3.7 million was offered by the government for those who can help in the apprehension of the suspects.
Jumalon was gunned down last November in Calamba, Misamis Occidental while staging his live broadcast inside his residence.
Jumalon was the owner and anchor of 94.7 Gold FM community radio station in Calamba, Misamios Occidental.

headline: Bulacan Grotto gears up for surge of Lenten pilgrims | ABS-CBN News
text:
SAN JOSE DEL MONTE, Bulacan – Preparations are underway for the expected surge of pilgrims at Our Lady of Lourdes Grotto Shrine, a popular religious destination in San Jose Del Monte City in Bulacan province, as the Holy Week draws near.
The grotto shrine, colloquially called Grotto, is often visited by Roman Catholics during the Lenten season amid its religious features, such as basilica, holy spring water, calvary hill, and rosary hill, among others.
The basilica is a replica of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Rosary in Lourdes, France.
Noreen Blanco, among the descendants of Horacio Guanzon and Anita Guidote Guanzon who established the Grotto in 1965 and currently manages the 25-hectare land, said over a million visitors are expected to surge in the coming Holy Week, from March 24 to 31.
“It’s because I guess we’ve always have the reputation that it’s a place where people get healed,” Blanco told ABS-CBN News on Wednesday.
The Grotto features a holy spring water believed to be miraculous and can cure diseases. It is free to anyone.
“Para mainom para sa mga karamdaman,” said Letty Vicente after fetching liters of water on Wednesday.
“Depende kasi sa tao kung naniniwala ka, pero kung kulang ka naman paniniwala, parang wala din. Kailangan may kasamang panalangin,” she added.
Cedeie Rosendo, Grotto’s maintenance supervisor, said the management is currently coordinating with the local government unit for the deployment of police and rescue and medical personnel.
Some non-government organizations are also expected to volunteer to ensure an orderly observance of Holy Week.
A clean-up drive is also ongoing.
“Mensahe ko lang sa mga gustong pumunta sa amin sa Grotto, huwag po tayong magkalat kung saan-saan. Makipagtulungan na lang po sa amin,” Rosendo said.
Blanco said the family is currently in the process of donating the basilica to the Diocese of Malolos.
“Once it’s been donated to them, the arrangement is their going to manage the place,” she said.
